content: Codex CLI has an internal "memory writing agent" in the Rust binary at `codex_core::memories::phase1` and `phase2` that fires BACKGROUND model calls separate from the user's primary thread call. Its model is hardcoded to `gpt-5.1-codex-mini`. On ChatGPT-subscription tier auth, that specific model is not supported, so the memory-agent call returns `{"detail":"The 'gpt-5.1-codex-mini' model is not supported when using Codex with a ChatGPT account."}` — and this error propagates up through the SDK as if it came from the primary review call, even though the primary call asked for a different model. Surface symptoms: error message quotes a model name the caller never configured, and generic fallback tips (e.g., "set model to gpt-5.4") are useless because the caller is already on their configured model. To diagnose, preserve and inspect the raw error (we already do, via the `Original error:` suffix added in commit 2f17aa2 for ISS-001). Mitigations: disable Codex memories if the CLI exposes a flag, use API-key auth (bypasses the ChatGPT-tier model restrictions), or surface a distinct tip when the extracted model differs from the caller's configured model.
context: Debugged 2026-04-23 via `strings` on `~/.nvm/versions/node/v22.18.0/lib/node_modules/@openai/codex/bin/codex` — found `gpt-5.1-codex-mini` and `codex_core::memories::phase1::job` / `phase2::agent` in the binary. Reproduced live: review configured for gpt-5.4 failed with MODEL_ERROR quoting gpt-5.1-codex-mini. Filed as ISS-003. Supersedes any prior assumption that errors with a quoted model name always refer to the user-configured model for the review thread.
title: Codex CLI memory-writing agent uses hardcoded gpt-5.1-codex-mini
